title: Pub / Counter Height Table
summary: A big square slat-top table on four 4x4 legs — seventeen tabletop boards and a mitered corner brace at each underside corner.
description:
This is a large, nearly-square pub table built the same way a deck or porch floor is: a square apron frame on four legs, with support boards running across the middle so seventeen individual 1x4 tabletop boards have something to land on every few feet rather than spanning unsupported. Screwing down through each tabletop board into both the apron and the support boards underneath is what keeps a slat top like this from cupping or working loose over time — don't skip the extra screws into the support boards just because the apron screws feel like enough.

The corner braces (mitered at 45° and screwed into the underside corners) aren't decorative — a big unsupported square frame like this wants to rack into a parallelogram under lateral push, and the corner braces are what stop it.

tools.note: Cuts the 45° corner brace ends and the leg lengths.

cutList.note: 36" suits both pub and counter height — adjust to your preference before cutting.

steps.title: Build the apron frame
steps.body: Connect one long apron and one short apron perpendicular at the ends to form an L-shape, using 1-1/2" screws and glue. Repeat with the remaining long and short apron to make a second L. Join the two L-shapes together to form a square frame with 1-1/2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach a leg to the apron frame at each corner, screwing into both apron faces that meet at that corner, using 2" screws and glue. Repeat for all four legs.

steps.title: Add the mid supports
steps.body: On the long aprons, mark 18" in from each end and attach a mid support board there with 2" screws and glue. Repeat for the second support.

steps.title: Attach the tabletop boards
steps.body:
Attach the first tabletop board perpendicular to the short aprons and support boards, square to the apron edge, using 1-1/2" screws and glue. Drive one or two additional screws into each support board through the tabletop for a total of 6–8 screws per board. Repeat for all seventeen tabletop boards, spacing them evenly across the frame.

Cut a 45° angle on both ends of each 12" corner brace and attach to the underside corners of the table, screwing from the apron into the brace. Repeat at all four corners — these keep the frame from racking.

images.alt: Square pub-height table with a slat top on four 4x4 legs
